  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "A review should assess whether the section clearly frames the system’s thesis, motivation, and contribution at a conceptual level.",
    "R1.2": "The review needs to check that the reasoning from operational problem to design to claimed performance benefits is logically coherent.",
    "R1.3": "Because the text makes quantitative and causal claims, the review should evaluate whether these are supported by the described evaluation and evidence as presented.",
    "R1.5": "Section-level reviewing includes assessing whether the ordering and narrative arc (motivation, design, evaluation pointers, summary) aid reader comprehension.",
    "R1.6": "The request implies attention to clarity, grammar, and concision at the sentence and paragraph level.",
    "RX.1": "The reviewer should disclose uncertainties arising from only having this section and not the full paper context.",
    "RX.2": "The review must avoid overstating judgments, especially regarding causality and novelty beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "Scope should be limited to reviewing this section rather than proposing experiments or redesigning the system.",
    "RX.4": "Feedback should avoid encouraging practices that could cause misuse or unsafe deployment implications.",
    "RX.5": "Comments should be tied to specific sentences or claims in the provided text to ensure traceability."
  }
}

rationale.R1.1: A review should assess whether the section clearly frames the system’s thesis, motivation, and contribution at a conceptual level.
rationale.R1.2: The review needs to check that the reasoning from operational problem to design to claimed performance benefits is logically coherent.
rationale.R1.3: Because the text makes quantitative and causal claims, the review should evaluate whether these are supported by the described evaluation and evidence as presented.
rationale.R1.5: Section-level reviewing includes assessing whether the ordering and narrative arc (motivation, design, evaluation pointers, summary) aid reader comprehension.
rationale.R1.6: The request implies attention to clarity, grammar, and concision at the sentence and paragraph level.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer should disclose uncertainties arising from only having this section and not the full paper context.
rationale.RX.2: The review must avoid overstating judgments, especially regarding causality and novelty beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: Scope should be limited to reviewing this section rather than proposing experiments or redesigning the system.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should avoid encouraging practices that could cause misuse or unsafe deployment implications.
rationale.RX.5: Comments should be tied to specific sentences or claims in the provided text to ensure traceability.
